topic: laparoscopic cholecystectomy complete surgical process diagram

review_scale.wrong: Visible but anatomically wrong, unsafe, or misleading; state the correct relationship.
review_scale.needs_manual_verification: Suspicious or borderline; a human expert should confirm.
review_scale.cannot_verify: Image too unclear or occluded to judge; verify manually.
review_scale.not_applicable: Not required for this specific requested figure.

results.item: Gallbladder fundus, body, infundibulum/neck, and inferior liver edge.
Cystic duct, common hepatic duct, common bile duct, cystic artery, and right hepatic artery relationship.
Hepatocystic triangle / Calot triangle shown as a real anatomic space, not a decorative outline.
Critical View of Safety panel with lower gallbladder separated from the liver bed.
Cystic duct joins the common hepatic duct to form the common bile duct.
Cystic artery lies in or near the hepatocystic triangle and approaches the gallbladder neck.
Only cystic duct and cystic artery should enter the gallbladder before division.
Common bile duct remains continuous and untouched in clip/divide panels.
Common bile duct clipped or divided.
Cystic duct confused with common bile duct.
A third duct shown entering the gallbladder in the CVS panel.
Cystic artery placed far outside the hepatocystic triangle without explanation.
Port/instrument context and gross anatomy.
Gallbladder exposure and fundus/infundibulum retraction to open the hepatocystic triangle.
Dissection and clearing of the hepatocystic / Calot triangle.
Critical View of Safety verification: only cystic duct and cystic artery enter the gallbladder; lower gallbladder separated from the liver bed.
Clip and divide the cystic duct and cystic artery; common bile duct untouched.
Gallbladder separation from the liver bed and specimen extraction.
Cystic duct joins the common hepatic duct to form the common bile duct; CBD continuous and uninstrumented.
Cystic artery lies within the hepatocystic triangle.
CVS panel shows only two structures entering the gallbladder and the lower third off the liver bed.
No extra/third duct entering the gallbladder.
Non-gory and publication-appropriate.
